name: design-craft
description: Turn Claude into an opinionated, accessibility-aware, AI-slop-resistant design collaborator that produces intentional design artifacts in HTML/CSS/SVG/JS — landing pages, interactive prototypes, mobile app screens, slide decks, wireframes, hi-fi variations, design-token extractions, and component inventories. The SKILL.md carries a full design philosophy (content discipline, purposeful aesthetics that reject AI-template tropes, visual hierarchy and rhythm, typography and color systems, interface copy as design material, WCAG accessibility, interaction states, system thinking, respecting the medium) and routes to 28 phased procedures across production (discovery-questions, frontend-aesthetic-direction, wireframe, make-a-deck, make-a-prototype, make-tweakable, generate-variations, make-an-animation, make-a-doc, generate-images), craft (motion-design, gsap-motion, depth-and-3d, laws-of-composition, typesetting, data-viz, mobile-design), system (design-system-extract, component-extract, design-system-author), and review (unit-critique-gate, accessibility-audit, ai-slop-check, hierarchy-rhythm-review, interaction-states-pass, visual-verification, polish-pass). Built for Claude Code: AskUserQuestion for kickoff rounds, the Agent tool for parallel verifier fan-out, and self-contained deck-scaling / device-frame / tweak-panel code that runs as standalone files. v1.2 adds mobile-design (iOS/Material platform grammar, thumb zone, input methods, industry conventions, emotional design), gsap-motion (timelines, ScrollTrigger scroll storytelling, SplitText, SVG draw/morph, Flip/Draggable — all plugins free via CDN), visual-verification (viewport-matrix layout integrity + screenshot playbook, a fifth polish-pass reviewer), an interface-copy chapter, subject-derived aesthetics with a plan-time signature element and swap-test genericness gate, and detection for all three current AI-default looks (warm-editorial, dark+acid-accent, broadsheet). v1.3 wires in local design-system libraries (open-design DESIGN.md format: brand systems as binding context, library systems as direction candidates, deck-template styles for deck variety), portable 9-section DESIGN.md emission from design-system-extract/author plus signature-moves capture, accent budgets written as enforceable usage rules, Karpathy-style surgical iteration discipline, and an interface-copy lens in the polish-pass jury. v1.4 adds an agentic-iteration layer — unit-critique-gate (a mid-build per-unit draft→critique→repair loop with a canonical scores+mustFix rubric, convergence budgets, and a don't-double-loop rule for orchestrated harnesses), a deterministic design-lint script (stdlib Python, runs in any seeded sandbox), an autonomous mode for orchestrated/headless runs (assumptions instead of questions, iterate harder), and per-unit visual micro-checks with honest degradation when no browser exists — plus the typesetting and data-viz craft references and the ux-craft companion skill as a standing dependency for flows, forms, and UX review. v1.5 adds a redesign procedure (references/redesign.md: preserve/overhaul mode detection, audit-before-touching incl. SEO/analytics dependencies, a never-change-silently list, and modernisation levers in priority order), optical/cross-card alignment checks in hierarchy-rhythm-review, logo-wall sourcing (Simple Icons / SVG monograms, logos-only) and bento background-diversity rules in ai-slop-check, the double-bezel nested-enclosure technique in depth-and-3d, and a deliver-the-whole-count rule for multi-unit briefs — selected synthesis from the taste-skill corpus. v1.6 hardens verification into an epistemics rather than a checklist: visual-verification gains a Phase 0 verification contract (rendering a screenshot is not seeing one; ask each capture "what is wrong with this?" not "is this done?"; a gate is downstream of the findings that motivated it, so a clean lint means no known defect is present and never means verified; prove every new rule can fail by running it against the artifact that motivated it; coverage is silent because a selector matching nothing passes; crop to components at DPR 2-3 because a page thumbnail cannot resolve the defect; measure ink via a baseline probe plus canvas TextMetrics.actualBoundingBoxAscent rather than getBoundingClientRect boxes; and static checks are structurally blind to motion), a three-line report shape separating what a machine asserts from what you looked at from what you did not check, make-a-doc's print freeze reworked around the resting-style-is-final-style contract (invert the states when you own the CSS; the negative-delay skip is the fallback for stylesheets you don't), motion-design's review gaining the three-pass motion verification (print at rest, reduced-motion at rest, mid-flight frames) and an explicit z-index rule for transient overlays, and unit-critique-gate spelling out that a clean lint licenses one sentence and it is not "verified".
